CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RONALD D. FOREMAN OF FOREMAN & BRASSO, TEL. NO. 433-3475 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. SHAWNA KASHACK OF THE LAW FIRM OF FLESHER MCKAGUE, TEL. NO. (916)358-9042 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CENTRE FOR NEURO SKILLS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT CENTRE FOR NEURO SKILLS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT- The demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of the implied covenant is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-complainant can only recover contractual damages on this claim and can seek the same relief in the first cause of action for breach of contract. The demurrer to the third cause of action for Unfair Business Practices is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant should amend the claim to (1) clarify that he is seeking relief under the UCL (Business and Professions Code sec. 17200) rather than the UPA; and (2) allege whether cross-defendant is being sued for an unfair, unlawful, and/or fraudulent business practice. Cross-defendant's argument regarding whether Health and Safety Code sec. 127420 applies in this case raises a factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress is sustained with leave to amend to plead a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress and/or an independent duty arising from tort law. Cross-complainant fails to allege a duty independent of the contract. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for elder abuse is overruled because defendant raises factual issues. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RONALD D. FOREMAN OF FOREMAN & BRASSO, TEL. NO. 433-3475 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. TIMOTHY J. NALLY OF THE LAW FIRM OF LEWIS & BRISBOIS, TEL. NO. (916) 564-5400 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLDEN RULE INSURANCE COMPANY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLDEN RULE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 3RD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is denied. Cross-complainant's allegations of bad faith and malicious conduct support the allegations. Discovery will reveal which, if any, corporate officer, director, or managing agent acted maliciously. Defendant has leave to add allegations of bad faith and malicious conduct by corporate officers, directors or managing agents. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RONALD D. FOREMAN OF FOREMAN & BRASSO, TEL. NO. 433-3475 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. SHAWNA KASHACK OF THE LAW FIRM OF FLESHER MCKAGUE, TEL. NO. (916)358-9042 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CENTRE FOR NEURO SKILLS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT CENTRE FOR NEURO SKILLS MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend. Cross-complainant cannot recover damages or fees on the UCL (Business and Professions Code sec. 17200) claim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Defendant Gregory Olbright'S Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-11-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Friday, October 11, 2013:
Reid L. Steinfeld, Esq. of Grant & Weber [212.504.6680] appearing by telephone for plaintiff GRANT & WEBER;
No other appearances.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant GREGORY OLBRIGHT'S Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery is DENIED AS MOOT. Plaintiff's counsel served verified responses to the written discovery at issue in this motion (Defendant's Form Interrogatories - Set One and Defendant's Special Interrogatories - Set One) on August 29, 2013, two days after Defendant filed the motion to compel. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's counsel to prepare a form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst A. Halperin; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
